ddls:
CREATE TABLE "problems" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each problem */,
  "problem_description" TEXT /* Description of the statistical problem */,
  "bias" INTEGER /* Indicates if the problem creates bias (YES/NO) */,
  "affect_se" INTEGER /* Indicates if the problem affects statistical estimations (YES/NO) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the problem (e.g., data-related, model-related) */,
  "commonality" TEXT /* Commonality of the problem (e.g., rare, common, frequent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solutions" (
  "solution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solution */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the problem this solution addresses */,
  "solution_description" TEXT /* Description of the solution to the problem */,
  "implementation_difficulty" TEXT /* Implementation difficulty of the solution (e.g., easy, medium, hard) */,
  "effectiveness" REAL /* Effectiveness of the solution (0-1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("solution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solutions_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_designs" (
  "design_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research design */,
  "design_name" TEXT /* Name of the research design */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the research design */,
  "study_type" TEXT /* Study type (e.g., experimental, observational) */,
  "population_applicability" INTEGER /* Indicates if the design is applicable to the general population (YES/NO) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("design_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement method */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement (e.g., Direct, Indirect) */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the measurement method, expressed as a percentage */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the measurement method, expressed as a percentage */,
  "reliability" REAL /* Reliability of the measurement method, expressed as a percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistical_estimates" (
  "estimate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistical estimate */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the problem related to this estimate */,
  "estimate_value" REAL /* Value of the statistical estimate */,
  "confidence_interval" TEXT /* Confidence interval of the estimate */,
  "standard_error" REAL /* Standard error of the estimate */,
  "p_value" REAL /* p-value of the estimate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("estimate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistical_estimates_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, statistician, analyst) */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., university, organization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who owns the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_problems" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project that encountered the problem */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the problem encountered in the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the problem in the project context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id", "problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_solutions" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project that implemented the solution */,
  "solution_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the solution implemented in the project */,
  "implementation_date" TEXT /* Date the solution was implemented in the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id", "solution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who collaborated on the project */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the user collaborated on */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the project (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "project_id")
)
